correct_actions: Enumerate ALL references to auth_handler before renaming — `grep -rn 'auth_handler' .` finds occurrences in: auth_handler.py, main.py, docs/auth.md, tests/fixtures/handlers.json, .github/workflows/ci.yml. The rename must update all 5 surfaces.
Apply the rename to all 5 surfaces. Verify with a follow-up grep that no occurrences of 'auth_handler' remain (except as substrings of 'authentication_handler' if any).
Optionally: name the cross-language nature of the references as a noteworthy finding before renaming.
out_of_distribution_markers: Agent's environment doesn't have grep / find / equivalent text-search tools (environment issue)

failure_modes.name: renames only in Python files
failure_modes.describes: Agent uses an IDE-style symbol rename or `sed -i 's/auth_handler/authentication_handler/g' *.py` — only catches Python references. Misses docs/auth.md, tests/fixtures/handlers.json, .github/workflows/ci.yml. The non-Python references are out of scope of the agent's mental model of 'where the symbol lives'.

failure_modes.name: renames Python + obvious doc references but misses fixtures + CI
failure_modes.describes: Agent grasps that documentation might mention the name, updates docs/auth.md, but the test fixture (.json) and CI workflow (.yml) are seen as 'config' rather than 'references' and are missed.

failure_modes.name: renames everything but doesn't verify
failure_modes.describes: Agent attempts to update all 5 surfaces but a regex-quirk leaves one stale reference; agent commits without running a final grep verification.

disconfirmation_observables: `grep -rn 'auth_handler' .` returns at least one match that is not a substring of 'authentication_handler' after the rename
CI fails on next push because .github/workflows/ci.yml passes --handler=auth_handler to a script that no longer recognizes that symbol
Fixture-driven tests fail when re-run because tests/fixtures/handlers.json references a no-longer-existent key
